question_en: In Wales, many people speak Gaelic – a completely different language from English – and it is taught in schools and universities:
explanation_en: False .﻿In Wales, many people speak Welsh – a completely different language from English – and it is taught in schools and universities.

question_en: What TWO films were directed by David Lean?
explanation_en: ﻿David Lean directed Brief Encounter in 1945 and Lawrence of Arabia in 1962 .

question_en: Which religious community celebrates Vaisakhi?
explanation_en: Vaisakhi (also spelled Baisakhi) is a Sikh festival which celebrates the founding of the Sikh community known as the Khalsa.

question_en: Which book written by JRR Tolkien was voted the country’s best-loved novel in 2003?
explanation_en: In 2003, The Lord of the Rings by JRR Tolkien was voted the country’s best-loved novel.

question_en: Which Anglo-Saxon poem is about a hero’s battle against monsters and has been translated into modern English?
explanation_en: The Anglo-Saxon poem Beowulf tells of its hero’s battles against monsters and is still translated into modern English.

question_en: Which of the following movies was directed by Alfred Hitchcock in 1935?
explanation_en: The 39 Steps (1935) was directed by Alfred Hitchcock.

question_en: What is the note with the highest value in the UK?
explanation_en: The note with the highest value in the UK is the £50 note.

question_en: Which is the most famous tennis tournament hosted in Britain and which takes place each year at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club?
explanation_en: The most famous tournament hosted in Britain is The Wimbledon Championships , which takes place each year at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club.

question_en: What name is given to the clock tower at the Houses of Parliament in honour of Queen Elizabeth II’s Diamond Jubilee in 2012?
explanation_en: The clock tower at the Houses of Parliament in London is named ‘Elizabeth Tower’ in honour of Queen Elizabeth II’s Diamond Jubilee in 2012.

question_en: Which British actor has recently won an Oscar?
explanation_en: Recent British actors to have won Oscars include Colin Firth, Sir Antony Hopkins , Dame Judi Dench, Kate Winslet and Tilda Swinton.

question_en: What TWO names are given to the day before Lent?
explanation_en: The day before Lent starts is called Shrove Tuesday, or Pancake Day .

question_en: Which of the following landmarks is the largest expanse of fresh water in mainland Britain?
explanation_en: Loch Lomond is the largest expanse of fresh water in mainland Britain and probably the best-known part of the park.

question_en: Who wrote the music for the popular show Jesus Christ Superstar?

options.en: Hans Holbein and Sir Anthony Van Dyck

explanation_en: Andrew Lloyd Webber has written the music for shows which have been popular throughout the world, including, in collaboration with Tim Rice, Jesus Christ Superstar and Evita, and also Cats and The Phantom of the Opera.

question_en: Pantomimes are a British tradition that many theatres produce during:
explanation_en: Many theatres produce a pantomime at Christmas time.

question_en: What is the minimum age requirement to buy alcohol in a pub or night club in the UK?
explanation_en: ﻿To buy alcohol in a pub or night club you must be 18 or over , but people under that age may be allowed in some pubs with an adult. When they are 16, people can drink wine or beer with a meal in a hotel or restaurant (including eating areas in pubs) as long as they are with someone over 18.

question_en: What is the coin with the highest value in the UK?
explanation_en: ﻿The coin with the highest value in the UK is worth £2 .

question_en: Which TWO of the following are famous horse racing events in the UK?
explanation_en: ﻿ The Grand National and Royal Ascot are famous horse racing events that take place in the UK.

question_en: Who wrote an oratorio called ‘Messiah’, which is regularly sung by choirs at Easter time?
explanation_en: ﻿George Frederick Handel wrote an oratorio, Messiah, which is sung regularly by choirs, often at Easter time.

question_en: Which of the following is England’s largest national park?
explanation_en: ﻿The Lake District is England’s largest national park, covering 912 square miles (2,362 square kilometres).

question_en: Which of the following is a traditional English food?
explanation_en: ﻿ Roast beef is a traditional English food, which is served with potatoes, vegetables, Yorkshire puddings (batter that is baked in the oven) and other accompaniments.

question_en: What are pantomimes based on?
explanation_en: Pantomimes are based on fairy stories and are light-hearted plays with music and comedy, enjoyed by family audiences.

question_en: Who was the first person to sail single-handed around the world in 1966/67?
explanation_en: A British sailor, Sir Francis Chichester , was the first person to sail singlehanded around the world, in 1966/67.

question_en: Who led the group of Catholics who tried to kill the Protestant king with a bomb in the Houses of the Parliament in 1605?
explanation_en: In 1605, a group of Catholics led by Guy Fawkes failed in their plan to kill the Protestant king with a bomb in the Houses of Parliament. This is the origin of the Bonfire Night celebration on the 5th November, when people in Great Britain set off fireworks at home or in special displays.

question_en: The UK continues to be a world leader in the development and manufacture of motor-sport technology:
explanation_en: True . There is a long history of motor sport in the UK, for both cars and motor cycles. Motor-car racing in the UK started in 1902. The UK continues to be a world leader in the development and manufacture of motor-sport technology.

question_en: Where is the Scottish Exhibition and Conference Centre (SEC Centre) located?
explanation_en: The Scottish Exhibition and Conference Centre (SEC Centre) is in Glasgow .

question_en: Which is the capital city of Northern Ireland?
explanation_en: The capital city of Northern Ireland is Belfast .

question_en: What name is given to the day when people play jokes on each other until midday?
explanation_en: April Fool’s Day , 1 April, is a day when people play jokes on each other until midday. The television and newspapers often have stories that are April Fool jokes.

question_en: What is Dame Ellen MacArthur known for?

options.en: She was the fastest person to have sailed around the world single-handed
She won the London Marathon six times
She won gold medals for ice dancing at the Olympic Games in 1984
She won gold medals in rowing in five consecutive Olympic Games

explanation_en: Dame Ellen MacArthur (1976-) is a yachtswoman and in 2004 became the fastest person to sail around the world singlehanded .

question_en: What are Welsh cakes made of?

options.en: Bacon, eggs, sausage, black pudding and potato bread

explanation_en: Welsh cakes are a traditional Welsh snack made from flour, dried fruits and spices , and served either hot or cold.

question_en: Where is the London Eye situated?

options.en: On the northern bank of the River Thames
On the southern bank of the River Thames
On the northern bank of the River Lea
On the southern bank of the River Lea

explanation_en: The London Eye is situated on the southern bank of the River Thames and is a Ferris wheel that is 443 feet (135 metres) tall.

question_en: In the UK, many people continue to visit the countryside for holidays and for leisure activities such as  walking, camping and fishing:
explanation_en: True . Many people continue to visit the countryside for holidays and for leisure activities such as walking, camping and fishing.

question_en: How often does the Edinburgh Festival take place?
explanation_en: The Edinburgh Festival takes place in Edinburgh, Scotland, every summer .

question_en: What name is given to the day that marks the end of the Ramadan, when Muslims have fasted for a month?
explanation_en: Eid a-Fitr celebrates the end of Ramadan, when Muslims have fasted for a month.

question_en: Who is the fastest person to have sailed around the world single-handed?
explanation_en: Dame Ellen MacArthur is a yachtswoman and in 2004 became the fastest person to sail around the world single-handed.

question_en: Where does the most famous sailing event in the UK take place?

options.en: Cowes, in the Isle of Wight
Douglas, in the Isle of Man
Plymouth, in the South West of England

explanation_en: The most famous sailing event in the UK takes place at Cowes on the Isle of Wight .

question_en: What is Sir Jackie Stewart famous for?

options.en: He captained the English football team that won the World Cup in 1966
He captained the English cricket team
He won the Formula 1 world championship three times
He won gold medals in rowing in five consecutive Olympic Games

explanation_en: Sir Jackie Stewart (1939-) is a Scottish former racing driver who won the Formula 1 world championship three times .

question_en: How old do you need to be to be able to go into betting shops or gambling clubs?
explanation_en: You have to be 18 to go into betting shops or gambling clubs.

question_en: How long do Muslims fast during Ramadan?
explanation_en: Eid a-Fitr celebrates the end of Ramadan, when Muslims have fasted for a month .

question_en: Which British actor has recently won an Oscar?
explanation_en: Recent British actors to have won Oscars include Colin Firth , Sir Antony Hopkins, Dame Judi Dench, Kate Winslet and Tilda Swinton.

question_en: When is St Andrews Day, patron of the Scotland, celebrated?
explanation_en: ﻿St Andrews Day, patron of the Scotland, is celebrated on the 30th of November .

question_en: Which organisation has been organising ‘The Proms’ since 1927?
explanation_en: The Proms is an eight-week summer season of orchestral classical music that takes place in various venues, including the Royal Albert Hall in London. It has been in various venues, including the Royal Albert Hall in London. It has been organised by the British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) since 1927.

question_en: Where is Snowdonia National Park located?
explanation_en: Snowdonia is a national park in North Wales .

question_en: Northern Ireland and Wales have their own banknotes, which are valid everywhere in the UK. However, shop and businesses do not have to accept them:
explanation_en: False . Northern Ireland and Scotland have their own banknotes, which are valid everywhere in the UK. However, shops and businesses do not have to accept them.

question_en: How many Formula 1 Grand Prix events are held in the UK every year?
explanation_en: A Formula 1 Grand Prix event is held in the UK each year and a number of British Grand Prix drivers have won the Formula 1 World Championship.

question_en: Which TWO British areas have their own banknotes?
explanation_en: Northern Ireland and Scotland have their own banknotes, which are valid everywhere in the UK. However, shops and businesses do not have to accept them.

question_en: How many weeks does the orchestral classical music event known as ‘the Proms’ last for?
explanation_en: The Proms is an eight-week summer season of orchestral classical music that takes place in various venues, including the Royal Albert Hall in London.

question_en: What is the name of the land formation consisting of columns made of volcanic lava located on the north-east coast of Northern Ireland?
explanation_en: Located on the north-east coast of Northern Ireland, the Giant’s Causeway is a land formation of columns made from volcanic lava.

question_en: What is the capital city of Wales?
explanation_en: The capital city of Wales is Cardiff .

question_en: When were the Houses of the Parliament built?
explanation_en: The Houses of Parliament and St Pancras Station were built in the 19th century , as were the town halls in cities such as Manchester and Sheffield.

question_en: How many times have the Olympic Games been hosted in the UK?
explanation_en: The UK has hosted the Olympic games on three occasions: 1908, 1948 and 2012 .

question_en: When were films first shown publicly in the UK?
explanation_en: ﻿Films were first shown publicly in the UK in 1896 and film screenings very quickly became popular. From the beginning, British film makers became famous for clever special effects and this continues to be an area of British expertise.

question_en: How long does Diwali last for?
explanation_en: ﻿Diwali normally falls in October or November and lasts for five days .

question_en: What was the population of the UK in 2005?
explanation_en: The population of the UK in 2005 was just under 60 million .

question_en: What game do usually people play at Halloween?
explanation_en: During Halloween young people will often dress up in frightening costumes to play ‘trick or treat’ .

question_en: What is the most well-known gallery in Wales?
explanation_en: In the UK, some of the most well-known galleries are The National Gallery, Tate Britain and Tate Modern in London, the National Museum in Cardiff , and the National Gallery of Scotland in Edinburgh.

question_en: The Man Booker Prize for Fiction is awarded annually for the best fiction novel written by an author from:

options.en: The European Union, Zambia or Zimbabwe
The Commonwealth, the USA or Australia
The European Union, Canada or Australia

explanation_en: The Man Booker Prize for Fiction is awarded annually for the best fiction novel written by an author from the Commonwealth, Ireland or Zimbabwe . It has been awarded since 1968.

question_en: Which of the following statements regarding Gaelic language is TRUE?

options.en: In Wales, Gaelic is spoken in some parts of the coast
In Scotland, Gaelic is spoken everywhere
In Scotland, Gaelic is spoken in some parts of the Highlands and Islands
In Wales, Gaelic is the main language

explanation_en: In Scotland, Gaelic (again, a different language) is spoken in some parts of the Highlands and Islands, and in Northern Ireland some people speak Irish Gaelic.

question_en: Where was the main Olympic site for the 2012 Games?
explanation_en: The main Olympic site for the 2012 Games was in Stratford , East London.

question_en: Who can get a 50% discount on their TV licences?
explanation_en: People over 75 can apply for a free TV licence and blind people can get a 50% discount.

question_en: Which British city is famous for its celebration of Diwali?
explanation_en: There is a famous celebration of Diwali in Leicester .

question_en: Which of the following British actresses has NOT won an Oscar?
explanation_en: Recent British actors to have won Oscars include Colin Firth, Sir Antony Hopkins, Dame Judi Dench, Kate Winslet and Tilda Swinton. Emily Watson has not won an oscar .

question_en: Which of the following sports has a long association with royalty?
explanation_en: Horse racing has a long association with royalty.

question_en: Hanukkah is a Jewish festival created to remember the Jews’ struggle for religious freedom.
explanation_en: True . Hanukkah is in November or December and is celebrated for eight days. It is to remember the Jews’ struggle for religious freedom.

question_en: What is the capital city of the UK?
explanation_en: The capital city of the UK is London .

question_en: What is Hogmanay?

options.en: The equivalent of Christmas Day in Scotland
The equivalent of New Year’s Eve in Scotland
The equivalent of Christmas Day in Wales
The equivalent of New Year’s Eve in Wales

explanation_en: In Scotland, 31 December is called Hogmanay .

question_en: On Remembrance Day, there is a two-minute silence at 11 AM to commemorate those who died fighting during the First World War?
explanation_en: True . On Remembrance Day at 11.00 am there is a two-minute silence and wreaths are laid at the Cenotaph in Whitehall, London.

question_en: What is the distance between the north coast of Scotland and the south-west corner of England?
explanation_en: In the UK, the longest distance on the mainland is from John O’Groats on the north coast of Scotland to Land’s End in the south-west corner of England. It is about 870 miles (approximately 1,400 kilometres).

question_en: How many volunteers work for the National Trust?
explanation_en: There are now more than 61,000 volunteers helping to keep the National Trust running.

question_en: What was the population of the UK in 1851?
explanation_en: The population of the UK in 1851 was 20 million people .

question_en: Which British playwright had a great influence on the English language and invented many words that are still common today?
explanation_en: William Shakespeare had a great influence on the English language and invented many words that are still common today.

question_en: What do Lewis Hamilton, Jenson Button and Damon Hill have in common?

options.en: They have won the Formula 1 World Championship
They have won the Six Nations Championship

explanation_en: Lewis Hamilton, Jenson Button and Damon Hill have won the Formula 1 World Championship .

question_en: According to the 2011 census, what percentage of people said that they had no religion?
explanation_en: In the 2011 census, 25% of people said they had no religion.

question_en: What other name is given to Diwali, the religious celebration that celebrates the victory of good over evil and the gaining of knowledge?
explanation_en: Diwali normally falls in October or November and lasts for five days. It is often called the Festival of Lights .

question_en: Which country invented rugby?
explanation_en: Rugby originated in England in the early 19th century and is very popular in the UK today.

question_en: Who were the Huguenots?
explanation_en: Between 1680 and 1720 many refugees called Huguenots came from France. They were Protestants and had been persecuted for their religion.

question_en: Which British scientist showed that gravity applied to the whole universe?
explanation_en: Isaac Newton most famous published work was Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica (‘Mathematical Principle of Natural Philosophy’), which showed how gravity applied to the whole universe.

question_en: Which composer wrote music for King George I and for his son, George II?
explanation_en: George Frederick Handel wrote the Water Music for King George I and Music for the Royal Fireworks for his son, George II.

question_en: When did the English civil war start?
explanation_en: Civil war between the king and Parliament could not now be avoided and began in 1642 .

question_en: During the 19th century, the UK was the world’s major producer of which THREE materials?
explanation_en: British industry led the world in the 19th century. The UK produced more that half of the world’s iron, coal and cotton cloth .

question_en: Which TWO of the following British athletes have won gold medals in the Olympic Games?
explanation_en: Dame Kelly Holmes and Jessica Enis won gold medals in the Olympic Games.

question_en: The Romans remained in Britain for 500 years.
explanation_en: The Romans remained in Britain for 400 years .

question_en: Which country invaded the Falkland Islands in 1982?
explanation_en: In 1982, Argentina invaded the Falkland Islands, a British overseas territory in the South Atlantic.

question_en: Which TWO of the following are examples of criminal offences:

options.en: Cases of unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace
Selling tobacco to anyone under the age of 18
A dispute about faulty goods or services

explanation_en: Selling tobacco to anyone under the age of 18 and drinking in public are examples of criminal offences.

question_en: Which British landmark was built as part of the UK’s celebration of the new millenium?
explanation_en: The London Eye was originally built as part of the UK’s celebration of the new millennium and continues to be an important part of New Year celebrations.

question_en: Where did slaves mainly come from during the slave trade?
explanation_en: Slaves came primarily from West Africa .

question_en: Who introduced ‘shampooing’ in Britain?
explanation_en: Sake Dean Mahomet and his wife also introduced ‘shampooing’, the Indian art of head massage, to Britain.

question_en: To be able to vote in England, you have to register at your local council electoral registration office.
explanation_en: This statement is true.

question_en: Where can the Bayeux tapestry be seen?
explanation_en: The Battle of Hastings is commemorated in a great piece of embroidery, known as the Bayeux tapestry, which can still be seen in France today.

question_en: It is an offence not to have an MOT certificate if your vehicle is more than two years old or without car insurance.
explanation_en: It is an offence not to have an MOT certificate if your vehicle is more than THREE years old or without car insurance.

question_en: What was the Spanish Armada?
explanation_en: The Spanish Armada was a large fleet of ships , which had been sent by Spain to conquer England and restore Catholicism.

question_en: England was split into two groups during the English Civil War of 1642, which were known as:

options.en: The House of Lords and the House of Commons
The Beefeaters and the Yeoman Warders

explanation_en: The English Civil war began in 1642. The country split into those who supported the king (the Cavaliers) and those who supported Parliament (the Roundheads) .

question_en: What are the key roles of the governors and school boards? select THREE answers

options.en: Setting the strategic direction of the school

explanation_en: Governors and school boards have an important part to play in raising school standards. They have three key roles: setting the strategic direction of the school, ensuring accountability and monitoring and evaluating school performance .

question_en: A popular way for people to stay in touch with friends, organise social events, and share photos, videos and opinions is through social networking websites such as:
explanation_en: Social networking websites such as Facebook and Twitter are a popular way for people to stay in touch with friends, organise social events, and share photos, videos and opinions.

question_en: Why is it important to recycle (select two answers)?

options.en: Using recycled materials to make new products uses less energy than using raw materials
It is a criminal offence and you could be arrested if you do not do it
To reduce the expenses of the local council
Less rubbish is created, so the amount being put into landfill is reduced

explanation_en: It is important to recycle as much of your waste as you can. Using recycled materials to make new products uses less energy and means that we do not need to extract more raw materials from the earth. It also means that less rubbish is created, so the amount being put into landfill is reduced .

question_en: In Northern Ireland, a newly qualified driver must display an ____ plate for one year after passing the test.
explanation_en: In Northern Ireland, a newly qualified driver must display an ‘R’ plate (for restricted driver) for one year after passing the test.

question_en: When is the birth of Jesus Christ celebrated?
explanation_en: Christmas Day, 25 December , celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ.

question_en: Prince William is also known as:
explanation_en: Prince William is also known as the Prince of Wales .

question_en: How old do you need to be to be able to play the National Lottery?
explanation_en: People under 18 are not allowed to participate in the National Lottery.

question_en: Where is the five-day race meeting attended by members of the Royal Family and known as Royal Ascot celebrated?
explanation_en: Royal Ascot, the five-day race meeting  attended by members of the Royal Family, takes place in Berkshire.

question_en: Who was the first person to lead a Roman invasion in Britain in 55 BC?
explanation_en: Julius Caesar led a Roman invasion of Britain in 55 BC.

question_en: Who wrote ‘The Lord of the Rings’?
explanation_en: The Lord of the Rings was written by JRR Tolkien.

question_en: Which court deals with minor criminal cases in England, Wales and Northern Ireland?

options.en: The Justice of the Peace Court

explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, most minor criminal cases are dealt with in a Magistrates’ Court. In Scotland, minor criminal offences go to a Justice of the Peace Court.

question_en: What name is given to the tombs where people buried their dead during the Bronze Age?
explanation_en: During the Bronze Age people lived in roundhouses and buried their dead in tombs called round barrows .

question_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly cannot make decisions on:
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly can make decisions on issues such as education, agriculture, the environment, health and social services. It cannot make decisions on defence.

question_en: According to the 2011 Census, what percentage of the population identified themselves as Buddhist?
explanation_en: In the 2011 Census, 59% of people identified themselves as Christian. Much smaller proportions identified themselves as Muslim (4.8%), Hindu (1.5%), Sikh (0.8%), Jewish or Buddhist (both less than 0.5%).

question_en: In which category did Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean win gold medals at the Olympic Games in 1984?
explanation_en: Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean won gold medals for ice dancing at the Olympic Games in 1984 and is one of Britain’s greatest Olympians.

question_en: What is the first verse of the National Anthem of the UK ‘God save the King’?
explanation_en: The first verse of the National Anthem of the UK is ‘God save our gracious King!.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT a British invention of the 20th century?
explanation_en: Radioactivity is not a British invention.

question_en: Which British Prime Minister was famous for the speech ‘I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears and sweat’?
explanation_en: During the Second World War, Winston Churchill made many famous speeches including lines which you may still hear:  ‘I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears and sweat’. This was Churchill’s first speech to the House of Commons after he became Prime Minister, 1940.

question_en: Which of the following countries did NOT belong to the Allies during World War I?
explanation_en: Bulgaria was not part of the Allies during the First World War.

question_en: Which TWO of the following charities work with homeless people?
explanation_en: Charities that work with homeless people include Crisis and Shelter.

question_en: Which flower is associated with Scotland?
explanation_en: The thistle is the flower associated with Scotland.

question_en: What is the money from TV licences used for?

options.en: To pay for the British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC)

explanation_en: The money from TV licences is used to pay for the British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC). This is a public service broadcaster providing television and radio programmes. The BBC is the largest broadcaster in the world. It is the only wholly state-funded media organisation that is independent of government.

question_en: Members of the Welsh government are elected every four years on the basis of:

options.en: First past the post system (the candidate who gets the most votes)

explanation_en: The Welsh government and the Senedd are based in Cardiff, the capital city of Wales. There are 60 members of the Senedd (SMs) and elections are held every four years using a form of proportional representation.

question_en: What is the Commonwealth?

options.en: An association of countries that support each other and work together towards shared goals in democracy and development
An organisation responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in its member countries
An association of countries that promotes international peace and security
A group of European and North American countries that have agreed to help each other if they come under attack

explanation_en: The Commonwealth is an association of countries that support each other and work together towards shared goals in democracy and development. Most member states were once part of the British Empire, although a few countries which were not have also joined.

question_en: Which famous architect designed St Paul’s Cathedral after it was destroyed by a fire in 1666?
explanation_en: In 1666, a great fire destroyed much of the city of London, including many churches and St Paul’s Cathedral. London was rebuilt with a new St Paul’s, which was designed by a famous architect, Sir Christopher Wren.

question_en: Which of the following is a fundamental principle of life in the UK?

options.en: Intolerance of those with different beliefs

explanation_en: The fundamental principles of British life include: democracy, the rule of law, individual liberty, tolerance of those with different faiths and beliefs and participation in community life.

question_en: Which of the following countries belongs to the Commonwealth?
explanation_en: Mozambique.

question_en: Who has to pay national insurance contributions in the UK?

options.en: Almost everybody who is in paid work
People with earnings over £18,000 per year

explanation_en: Almost everybody in the UK who is in paid work, including self-employed people, must pay National Insurance Contributions.

question_en: A good way to support your local community is to shop for products locally.
explanation_en: This statement is TRUE.

question_en: Where is the centrepiece to the Remembrance Day service known as Cenotaph located?
explanation_en: The Cenotaph, the Centrepiece to the Remembrance Day service, is located in Whitehall, London.

question_en: Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) are elected on the basis of:

options.en: First past the post system (the candidate who gets the most votes)

explanation_en: Elections to the European Parliament use a system of proportional representation , where seats are allocated to each party in proportion to the total number of votes it has won.

question_en: The North Atlantic Treaty Organisation is a group of European and North American countries that was created with which TWO purposes:

options.en: Protect and promote human rights in those countries
Help each other if they come under attack
Maintain peace between all its members
Promote free trade amongst those countries

explanation_en: NATO is a group of European and North American countries that have agreed to help each other if they come under attack. It also aims to maintain peace between all of its members .

question_en: The small claims procedure is used in England and Wales for minor disputes for:
explanation_en: This procedure is used for claims of less than £10,000 in England and Wales and £3,000 in Scotland and Northern Ireland.

question_en: Which of the following is the responsibility of the Chancellor of the Exchequer?
explanation_en: Chancellor of the Exchequer is responsible for the economy .

question_en: Which of the following statements is true?

options.en: Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on television and published in official reports
Proceedings in Parliament are not broadcast on television but published in official reports
Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on the radio and not published in official reports
Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on the radio only

explanation_en: Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on television and published in official reports called Hansard.

question_en: The public can listen to debates in the Palace of Westminster from public galleries in the House of Commons but not in the House of Lords:
explanation_en: False . The public can listen to debates in the Palace of Westminster from public galleries in both the House of Commons and the House of Lords.

question_en: Which of the following organisations has no power to make laws but draws up conventions and charters, including the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms?
explanation_en: The Council of Europe is responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in those countries. It has no power to make laws but draws up conventions and charters, the most well-known of which is the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms, usually called the European Convention on Human Rights.

question_en: Which Court deals with the most serious criminal cases, such as murder, in Scotland?
explanation_en: The most serious criminal cases in Scotland, such as murder, are heard at a High Court with a judge and jury.

question_en: The leader of the opposition leads his or her party in pointing out what they see as the government’s (choose TWO options):
explanation_en: The leader of the opposition leads his or her party in pointing out what they see as the government’s failures and weaknesses .

question_en: What is the minimum age requirement to drive a mopped in the UK?
explanation_en: You need to be at least 16 years old to ride a moped, and there are other age requirements and special tests for driving large vehicles.

question_en: How many countries formed the European Economic Community (EEC) when it was first created in 1957?
explanation_en: The European Union (EU), originally called the European Economic Community (EEC), was set up by six western European countries (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands) who signed the Treaty of Rome on 25 March 1957.

question_en: What is donated blood used for?

options.en: To help people with a wide range of injuries and illnesses
Blood cannot be donated in the UK
To carry out research with your DNA
To be stored in the hospital in case you need it in the future

explanation_en: Donated blood is used by hospitals to help people with a wide range of injuries and illnesses .

question_en: In which city is the Northern Ireland Assembly building, also known as Stormont, located?
explanation_en: In Northern Ireland elected members, known as MLAs, meet in the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont, in Belfast .

question_en: What can living people donate that may help other people with injuries or illnesses (choose TWO options)?
explanation_en: Living people can donate blood and a kidney .

question_en: Where is the office of the MPs located?
explanation_en: The MP’s office is located at The House of Commons , Westminster, London, SW1A OAA.

question_en: Which TWO of the following statements is TRUE with regards to Magistrates and Justices of the Peace (JPs) in England, Wales and Scotland?

options.en: They do not need legal qualifications

explanation_en: In England, Wales and Scotland Magistrates and Justices of the Peace (JPs) usually work unpaid and do not need legal qualifications .

question_en: The House of Commons is normally more independent of the government than the House of Lords:
explanation_en: False . The House of Lords is normally more independent of the government than the House of Commons.

question_en: In Northern Ireland members of the Legislative Assembly (MLAs) are elected on the basis of:
explanation_en: ﻿The Northern Ireland Assembly has 90 elected members, known as MLAs (members of the Legislative Assembly). They are elected with a form of proportional representation .

question_en: How can MPs be contacted (choose TWO options)?

options.en: By going to your local council and asking for an appointment

explanation_en: MPs can be contacted by letter or telephone at their constituency office , or at their office in the House of Commons. In addition, many MPs, SMs and MSPs hold regular local ‘surgeries’, where constituents can go in person to talk about issues that are of concern to them.

question_en: Does Britain have a written constitution?
explanation_en: The British constitution is not written down in any single document, and therefore it is described as ‘unwritten’. This is mainly because the UK, unlike America or France, has never had a revolution which led permanently to a totally new system of government.

question_en: When were the United Nations set up?

options.en: Before the War of the Roses

explanation_en: The UN was set up after the Second World War and aims to prevent war and promote international peace and security.

question_en: Where can you find copies of the ‘Hansard’?

options.en: In large libraries and at www.parliament.uk

explanation_en: Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on television and published in official reports called Hansard. Written reports can be found in large libraries and at www.parliament.uk .

question_en: Which court normally deals with cases of children aged 10 to 17 in England, Wales and Northern Ireland?
explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, if an accused person is aged 10 to 17, the case is normally heard in a Youth Court in front of up to three specially trained magistrates or a District Judge. The most serious cases will go the Crown Court.

question_en: How many senior MPs are appointed by the Prime Minister?
explanation_en: The Prime Minister appoints about 20 senior MPs to become ministers in charge of departments.

question_en: How can you visit the Senedd?
explanation_en: To make a booking to visit the Senedd, you have to contact the Senedd Booking Service .

question_en: In which of the following matters the Scottish Parliament CANNOT legislate?
explanation_en: The matters on which the Scottish Parliament can legislate include: civil and criminal law, health, education, planning and additional tax-raising powers.

question_en: Does Britain have a constitutional monarchy?
explanation_en: True . The UK has a constitutional monarchy.

question_en: The devolved administrations of Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland each have their own civil service:
explanation_en: True . The devolved administrations each have their own civil service.

question_en: Carrying a weapon is a criminal offence, unless it is for self-defence:
explanation_en: False . It is a criminal offence to carry a weapon of any kind, even if it is for self-defence. This includes a gun, a knife or anything that is made or adapted to cause injury.

question_en: Prohibition of thought, conscience and religion is one of the principles included in the European Convention of Human Rights:
explanation_en: False . Freedom of thought, conscience and religion is one of the principles included in the European Convention of Human Rights

question_en: Will a non-UK national living in the UK and looking for work, starting work or setting up as self-employed need a National Insurance number?
explanation_en: True . A non-UK national living in the UK and looking for work, starting work or setting up as self-employed will need a National Insurance number. However, you can start work without one.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT a responsibility of the MPs?

options.en: Scrutinise and comment on what the government is doing

explanation_en: MPs have a number of different responsibilities. They represent everyone in their constituency, help to create new laws, scrutinize and comment on what the government is doing and debate important national issues.

question_en: The clock tower is named ‘Elizabeth Tower’ in honour of:

options.en: Queen Elizabeth II’s Diamond Jubilee in 2012
Queen Elizabeth I’s Diamond Jubilee in 2012
Queen Elizabeth II’s Golden Jubilee in 2002
Queen Elizabeth I’s Golden Jubilee in 2002

explanation_en: The clock tower is named ‘Elizabeth Tower’ in honour of Queen Elizabeth II’s Diamond Jubilee in 2012 .

question_en: The right to a fair trial is not included amongst the principles of the European Convention of Human Rights:
explanation_en: False . The right to a fair trial is one of the principles of the European Convention of Human Rights.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT classified as a criminal offence in the UK?

options.en: Selling alcohol to anyone under the age of 18
Unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace

explanation_en: Cases of unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace are considered civil offences.

question_en: The right to life and the right to liberty and security are some of the principles included in the European Convention of Human Rights:
explanation_en: This is true .

question_en: When are local elections for councillors held in most local authorities?
explanation_en: For most local authorities, local elections for councillors are held in May every year .

question_en: Which of the following is NOT protected by the laws of discrimination?
explanation_en: UK laws ensure that people are not treated unfairly in any area of life or work because of their age, disability, sex, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sexuality or marital status.

question_en: Which of the following countries is NOT a member of the Commonwealth?
explanation_en: Greece is not a member of the Commonwealth.

question_en: In England, Wales and Scotland, most minor criminal cases are dealt with in a Magistrates’ Court:
explanation_en: False . In England, Wales and Northern Ireland , most minor criminal cases are dealt with in a Magistrates’ Court. In Scotland, minor criminal offences go to a Justice of the Peace Court.

question_en: When did the UK sign the European Convention of Human Rights?
explanation_en: The UK was one of the first countries to sign the Convention in 1950 .

question_en: Which of the following is NOT a devolved administration?
explanation_en: There are three devolved administrations in the UK, this include: the Senedd, the Scottish Parliament and the Northern Ireland Assembly.

question_en: What was the name of the European Union when it was first established?
explanation_en: The European Union (EU), originally called the European Economic Community (EEC) , was set up by six western European countries (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands) who signed the Treaty of Rome on 25 March 1957.

question_en: Which Act incorporated the European Convention of Human Rights into UK law?
explanation_en: The Human Rights Act 1998 incorporated the European Convention of Human Rights into UK law.

question_en: Arranged marriages are not acceptable in the UK:
explanation_en: False . Arranged marriages, where both parties agree to the marriage, are acceptable in the UK.

question_en: Who represents the UK to the rest of the world?
explanation_en: The King represents the UK to the rest of the world.

question_en: Who is responsible for managing relationships with foreign countries?
explanation_en: The Foreign Secretary is responsible for managing relationships with foreign countries.

question_en: If a husband forces his wife to have sex, he can be charged with rape:
explanation_en: True . Any man who forces a woman to have sex, including a woman’s husband, can be charged with rape.

question_en: Where is the official home of the Prime Minister?
explanation_en: The official home of the Prime Minister is 10 Downing Street , in central London, near the Houses of Parliament. He or she also has a country house outside London called Chequers.

question_en: What name is given to the elected members of the European Parliament?
explanation_en: Elections for the European Parliament are held every five years. Elected members are called members of the European Parliament ( MEPs ).

question_en: Which court deals with cases involving personal injury, family matters, breaches of contract and divorce in England and Wales?
explanation_en: County Courts deal with a wide range of civil disputes. These include people trying to get back money that is owed to them, cases involving personal injury, family matters, breaches of contract, and divorce. In Scotland, most of these matters are dealt with in the Sheriff Court.

question_en: What is the name of the building where the Scottish Parliament meet?
explanation_en: In Scotland the elected members, called MSPs, meet in the Scottish Parliament building at Holyrood in Edinburgh.

question_en: Which court deals with minor criminal cases in England, Wales and Northern Ireland?

options.en: The Justice of the Peace Court

explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, most minor criminal cases are dealt with in a Magistrates’ Court . In Scotland, minor criminal offences go to a Justice of the Peace Court.

question_en: On which issues can the Northern Ireland Assembly NOT make decisions?
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly can make decisions on issues such as education, agriculture, the environment, health and social services. It cannot make decisions on defence .

question_en: What is the first verse of the National Anthem of the UK ‘God save the King’?
explanation_en: The first verse of the National Anthem of the UK is ‘God save our gracious King! .

question_en: Who forms the shadow cabinet?

options.en: Senior opposition MPs appointed by the leader of the opposition
Junior opposition MPs appointed by the leader of the governing party
Junior opposition MPs appointed by the leader of the opposition

explanation_en: The leader of the opposition appoints senior opposition MPs to be ‘shadow ministers’. They form the shadow cabinet and their role is to challenge the government and put forward alternative policies.

question_en: People in the UK have to pay tax on any earnings from (choose THREE options)?

options.en: Income from property, savings and dividends

explanation_en: People in the UK have to pay tax on their income, which includes: wages from paid employment, profits from self-employment, taxable benefits, pensions and income from property, savings and dividends .

question_en: How many devolved administrations are there in the UK?
explanation_en: There are three devolved administrations in the UK, this include: the Welsh government, the Scottish Parliament and the Northern Ireland Assembly.

question_en: Who is responsible for interpreting the law and ensuring that trials are conducted fairly?

options.en: The Prime Minister and the MPs

explanation_en: Judges (who are together called ‘the judiciary’ ) are responsible for interpreting the law and ensuring that trials are conducted fairly.

question_en: How often do people who are 70 years old or over need to renew their driving licence?
explanation_en: Drivers can use their driving licence until they are 70 years old. After that, the licence is valid for three years at a time.

question_en: Where are the Welsh government and the Senedd based?
explanation_en: The Welsh government and the Senedd are based in Cardiff , the capital city of Wales.

question_en: Which of the following is not a member of the European Union?
explanation_en: Norway is not a member of the European Union.

question_en: In the UK, alcohol cannot be sold to anyone under the age of:
explanation_en: It is a criminal offence to sell alcohol to anyone who is under 18 or to buy alcohol for people who are under the age of 18. (There is one exception: people aged 16 or over can drink alcohol with a meal in a hotel or restaurant.

question_en: To whom do new citizens have to swear or affirm loyalty as part of the citizenship ceremony?
explanation_en: New citizens swear or affirm loyalty to the King as part of the citizenship ceremony.

question_en: How can you visit the UK Parliament (choose TWO options)?

options.en: Arranging a tour through the visitor services
Writing to your local MP to ask for tickets
Queueing on the day at the public entrance

explanation_en: To visit the UK Parliament, you can write to your local MP in advance to ask for tickets or you can Queue on the day at the public entrance .

question_en: Forced marriage is where one or both parties do not or cannot give their consent to enter into the partnership:
explanation_en: True . Forced marriage is where one or both parties do not or cannot give their consent to enter into the partnership.

question_en: Which of the following drugs is illegal in the UK?

options.en: All of the above are illegal

explanation_en: Drugs such as heroin, cocaine, ecstasy and cannabis are illegal in the UK.

question_en: What is the name of the building where the Northern Ireland Assembly meet?
explanation_en: In Northern Ireland elected members, known as MLAs, meet in the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont , in Belfast.

question_en: Which of the following countries is NOT a member of the Commonwealth?
explanation_en: Zimbabwe is not a member of the Commonwealth.

question_en: Who makes the decisions on government policies in the UK?

options.en: Only the Prime Minister can make the decisions on government policies
The Prime Minister and the Cabinet
The monarch and the Prime Minister

explanation_en: Decisions on government policies are made by the Prime Minister and cabinet .

question_en: How often is the electoral register updated?
explanation_en: The electoral register is updated every year in September or October .

question_en: What do you need to be able to drive a car in the UK?

options.en: A driving licence, a valid motor insurance, a MOT certificate and the road tax
A driving licence and a valid motor insurance only
A driving licence and a MOT certificate only

explanation_en: To drive a car in the UK you need a driving licence, a valid motor insurance, a MOT certificate (for cars over three years old) and the road tax .

question_en: How many members does the Scottish Parliament have?
explanation_en: There are 129 members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs), elected by a form of proportional representation.

question_en: What does the term ‘coalition’ refer to?

options.en: If no political party wins a majority, up to three parties may join and govern together
The candidate who gets the most votes is elected
If no political party wins a majority, two parties may join and govern together
If an MP dies or resigns, there will be a fresh election

explanation_en: The government is usually formed by the party that wins the majority of constituencies. If no party wins a majority, two parties may join together to form a coalition .

question_en: How much do you have to pay to visit the Palace of Westminster?

options.en: The entrance to the Palace of Westminster is free

explanation_en: The entrance to the Palace of Westminster is free .

question_en: Civil servants cannot stand for public office:
explanation_en: True . Most citizens of the UK, the Irish republic or the Commonwealth aged 18 or over can stand for public office. There are some exceptions, including: members of the armed forces, civil servants, people found guilty of certain criminal offences.

question_en: What is the main function of the Council of Europe?

options.en: Recommend action when there are international crises and threats to peace

explanation_en: ﻿The Council of Europe has 47 member countries, including the UK, and is responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in those countries.

question_en: In which of the following areas has the Welsh government the power to make laws (choose four)?
explanation_en: The Welsh government has the power to make laws in 20 areas, including: education and training, health and social services, economic development and housing .

question_en: How many members comprise the UN Security Council?
explanation_en: There are 15 members on the UN Security Council, which recommends action when there are international crises and threats to peace.

question_en: Members in the Senedd can speak English and Welsh, but all publications of the Senedd must be in English:
explanation_en: False . Members in the Senedd can speak either in English or Welsh, and all of the Senedd\'s publications are in both languages.

question_en: How many members does the Northern Ireland Assembly have?
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly has 90 elected members , known as MLAs (Members of the Legislative Assembly). They are elected with a form of proportional representation.

question_en: Which of the following civil laws include disputes between landlords and tenants over issues such as repairs and eviction?
explanation_en: Housing law includes disputes between landlords and tenants over issues suck as repairs and eviction.

question_en: How can you visit the Scottish Parliament?

options.en: Arranging a tour through the visitor services

explanation_en: To visit the Scottish Parliament, you can get information, book tickets or arrange tours through visitor services . You can also write to them at the Scottish Parliament, Edinburgh, EH99 1SP, telephone 0131 348 5200 or email sp.booking@scottish.parliament.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT a duty of the police?
explanation_en: The job of the police in the UK is to protect life and property, prevent disturbances (also known as keeping the peace) and prevent and detect crime.

question_en: How often are the elections for the European Parliament held?
explanation_en: Elections for the European Parliament are held every five years .

question_en: What do new citizens have to swear or affirm as part of the citizenship ceremony?

options.en: They will remain in the UK
They will go to church at least once a week

explanation_en: New citizens swear or affirm loyalty to the King as part of the citizenship ceremony.

question_en: In which TWO cases may a person who has been summoned to do jury service be exempted from doing it:

options.en: If they have a criminal conviction
If they provide a good reason to be excused, such as ill health
If they have to go to work

explanation_en: Everyone who is summoned to do jury service must do it unless they are not eligible: for example, because they have a criminal conviction or if they provide a good reason to be excused, such as ill health .

question_en: What is the role of the shadow cabinet?

options.en: To keep order during political debates to make sure the rules are followed
To challenge the government and put forward alternative policies
To point out what they see as the government’s failures and weaknesses

explanation_en: The role of the shadow cabinet is to challenge the government and put forward alternative policies .

question_en: If you are self-employed:

options.en: You need to pay your own tax
Tax is automatically taken from your earnings by a specialised agency
You don’t need to pay tax
You only needed to pay tax on 50% of your earnings

explanation_en: If you are self-employed, you need to pay your own tax through a system called ‘self-assessment’, which includes completing a tax return.

question_en: What name is given to the elected members of the Northern Ireland Assembly?
explanation_en: Members of the Northern Ireland Assembly are known as MLAs (members of the Legislative Assembly) .

question_en: Which senior MP is responsible for issues relating to crime, policing and immigration?
explanation_en: The Home Secretary is responsible for crime, policing and immigration.

question_en: How can a person be protected from being forced into a marriage?

options.en: By giving them a place to stay away from their family
Court orders can be obtained to protect a person from being forced into a marriage
By speaking to the person’s parents
There are no laws in the UK to avoid this

explanation_en: Court orders can be obtained to protect a person from being forced into a marriage , or to protect a person in a forced marriage.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT classified as a criminal offence in the UK?

options.en: Unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace

explanation_en: Unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace is classified as a civil offence.

question_en: Which of the following is NOT considered to be a main part of the British government?
explanation_en: The European Council is not part of the British government.

question_en: Who appoints the members of the Cabinet?
explanation_en: The Prime Minister (PM) is the leader of the political party in power. He or she appoints the members of the cabinet and has control over many important public appointments.

question_en: Which court usually deals with the MOST serious civil cases in Scotland?

options.en: The Court of Session in Edinburgh

explanation_en: More serious civil cases – for example, when a large amount of compensation is being claimed – are dealt with in the High Court of England, Wales and Northern Ireland. In Scotland, they are dealt with in the Court of Session in Edinburgh.

question_en: Which of the following statements is TRUE?

options.en: Forcing another person to marry is a civil offence
Forcing another person to marry is a criminal offence
Forcing another person to marry is not punished in the UK
Forcing another person to marry is common in the UK

explanation_en: Forcing another person to marry is a criminal offence.

question_en: Where do UK residents need to register their car or motorcycle?

options.en: At the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA)

explanation_en: If you are resident in the UK, your car or motor cycle must be registered at the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) .

question_en: Which court deals with serious criminal offences in Wales?
explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, serious criminal offences are tried in front of a judge and a jury in a Crown Court . In Scotland, serious cases are heard in a Sheriff Court with either a sheriff or a sheriff with a jury.

question_en: When were Protection Orders to protect a person from being forced into a marriage, or to protect a person in a forced marriage introduced for Scotland?
explanation_en: Forced Marriage Protection Orders were introduced in 2008 for England, Wales and Northern Ireland under the Forced Marriage (Civil Protection) Act 2007. Court orders can be obtained to protect a person from being forced into a marriage, or to protect a person in a forced marriage. Similar Protection Orders were introduced in Scotland in November 2011 .

question_en: Since when have hereditary peers lost the automatic right to attend the House of Lords?
explanation_en: ﻿Since 1999 , hereditary peers have lost the automatic right to attend the House of Lords.

question_en: What name is given to the elected members of the Welsh government?
explanation_en: There are 60 members of the Senedd (SMs) and elections are held every four years using a form of proportional representation.

question_en: How often do the ministers that form the Cabinet meet to make important decisions about government policy?
explanation_en: The cabinet is a committee which usually meets weekly and makes important decisions about government policy.

question_en: How many countries are members of the United Nations?
explanation_en: The UK is part of the United Nations (UN), an international organization with more than 190 countries as members.

question_en: Which of the following statements is true?

options.en: The police force is a public service that helps and protects everyone
The police force is a private service that helps and protects everyone
The police force is a private service in charge of rescuing people from dangerous situations
The police force is a public service that works to avoid road accidents

explanation_en: The police force is a public service that helps and protects everyone , no matter what their background or where they live.

question_en: What is the system by which the income tax is automatically taken from the employee’s income by their employer and paid directly to HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC)?
explanation_en: For most people, the right amount of income tax is automatically taken from their income from employment by their employer and paid directly to HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC), the government department that collects taxes. This system is called “Pay As You Earn” (PAYE) .

question_en: How many local authorities are there in London?
explanation_en: London has 33 local authorities , with the Greater London Authority and the Mayor of London coordinating policies across the capital.

question_en: Who is the head of the state in the UK?

options.en: Prince Philip, the Duke of Edinburgh

explanation_en: King Charles III is the head of state of the UK.

question_en: Members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs) are elected on the basis of:

options.en: First past the post system (the candidate who gets the most votes)

explanation_en: Members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs) are elected by a form of proportional representation .

question_en: The action of handing out leaflets in the street or knocking on people’s doors to ask for their political support is known as:
explanation_en: The action of handing out leaflets in the street or knocking on people’s doors to ask for their political support is known as canvassing .

question_en: Which of the following is a charity that works with old people?
explanation_en: Age UK is a charity that works with old people.

question_en: How many members does the Council of Europe have?
explanation_en: The Council of Europe is separate from the EU. It has 47 member countries , including the UK, and is responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in those countries.

question_en: In the UK, Members of the Parliament (MPs) are elected on the basis of:

options.en: First past the post system (the candidate who gets the most votes)

explanation_en: MPs are elected through a system called ‘first past the post’ . In  constituency, the candidate who gets the most votes is elected.

question_en: People in the UK do NOT have to pay tax on:
explanation_en: People in the UK have to pay tax on their income, which includes: wages from paid employment, profits from self-employment, taxable benefits, pensions and income from property, savings and dividends.

question_en: What Treaty established the European Economic Union in 1957?
explanation_en: The European Union (EU), originally called the European Economic Community (EEC), was set up by six western European countries (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands) who signed the Treaty of Rome on 25 March 1957.

question_en: When did hereditary peers lose the automatic right to attend the House of Lords?
explanation_en: Since 1999 , hereditary peers have lost the automatic right to attend the House of Lords. They now elect a few of their number to represent them in the House of Lords.

question_en: When did the King start reining?
explanation_en: The King has reigned since his mother’s death in September 2022 .

question_en: Members of the public are not allowed in Youth Courts, and the name or photographs of the accused young person cannot be published in newspapers or used by the media:
explanation_en: True . Members of the public are not allowed in Youth Courts, and the name or photographs of the accused young person cannot be published in newspapers or used by the media.

question_en: How do we call a set of principles by which a country is governed and which includes all of the institutions that are responsible for running the country and how their power is kept in check?
explanation_en: A constitution is a set of principles by which a country is governed. It includes all of the institutions that are responsible for running the country and how their power is kept in check.

question_en: ﻿What is the National Anthem of the UK?
explanation_en: The National Anthem of the UK is ‘God Save the king’ .

question_en: The Commonwealth has no power over its members and it cannot suspend their membership:
explanation_en: False . The Commonwealth has no power over its members, although it can suspend membership.

question_en: Which TWO of the following are environmental charities?
explanation_en: The National Trust and Friends of the Earth are environmental charities.

question_en: How often are elections held for the Senedd?
explanation_en: The Senedd has 60 Senedd members (SMs) and elections are held every four years using a form of proportional representation.

question_en: What is the name given to the person appointed by local authorities to be the ceremonial leader of a particular council?
explanation_en: Many local authorities appoint a mayor , who is the ceremonial leader of the council. In some towns, a mayor is elected to be the effective leader of the administration.

question_en: What is the meaning of the term ‘first past the post’?

options.en: In each constituency, the candidate who gets the most votes is elected
If no political party wins a majority, two parties may join and govern together
If an MP dies or resigns, there will be a fresh election
The Speaker will be chosen by other MPs in a secret ballot

explanation_en: MPs are elected through a system called ‘first past the post’. In each constituency, the candidate who gets the most votes is elected .

question_en: Which court deals with the MOST serious criminal cases in Scotland, such as murder?
explanation_en: In Scotland, serious cases are heard in a Sheriff Court with either a sheriff or a sheriff with a jury. The most serious cases in Scotland, such as murder, are heard at a High Court with a judge and jury.

question_en: Who is the ceremonial head of the Commonwealth?

options.en: The President of the United States
The Prime Minister of the UK

explanation_en: The King is the ceremonial head of the Commonwealth.

question_en: Who is the chief officer of the House of Commons?
explanation_en: The Speaker is the chief officer of the House of Commons.

question_en: What characterises the British constitution?

options.en: It was written by the Prime Minister
It was written by the King
It is not written down in any single document, and therefore it is described as ‘unwritten
It was written by Henry VIII

explanation_en: The British constitution is not written down in any single document, and therefore it is described as ‘unwritten’ .

question_en: Who forms the judiciary?
explanation_en: Judges (who are together called ‘the judiciary’) are responsible for interpreting the law and ensuring that trials are conducted fairly.

question_en: Which court deals with minor criminal offences in Scotland?

options.en: The Justice of the Peace Court

explanation_en: In Scotland, minor criminal offences go to a Justice of the Peace Court .

question_en: If an MP dies or resigns, there will be a fresh election, which is known as:
explanation_en: If an MP dies or resigns, there will be a fresh election, called a by-election , in his or her constituency.

question_en: A person who has not paid enough National Insurance contributions will not be able to receive certain contributory benefits including (choose TWO options):
explanation_en: Anyone who does not pay enough National Insurance Contributions will not be able to receive certain contributory benefits such as Jobseeker’s Allowance or a full state retirement pension .

question_en: How are life peers appointed?

options.en: By the monarch on the advice of the Prime Minister
By the Prime Minister on the advice of the King

explanation_en: Life peers are appointed by the monarch on the advice of the Prime Minister .

question_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, if an accused person is aged 18, the case will be heard in a Youth Court:
explanation_en: False . In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, if an accused person is aged 10 to 17, the case is normally heard in a Youth Court in front of up to three specially trained magistrates or a District Judge.

question_en: The UN was set up after the First World War and aims to prevent war and promote international peace and security:
explanation_en: False . The UN was set up after the Second World War and aims to prevent war and promote international peace and security.

question_en: What is played at important national occasions and at events attended by the King or the Royal Family?
explanation_en: The National Anthem of the UK is ‘God Save the King’ is played at important national occasions and at events attended by the King or the Royal Family.

question_en: How many members does a jury have in Scotland?
explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland a jury has 12 members, and in Scotland a jury has 15 members .

question_en: How can you visit the Northern Ireland Assembly (choose TWO options)?

options.en: Arranging a tour through the visitor services

explanation_en: There are two ways to arrange a visit to the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont. You can either contact the Education Service (details are on the Northern Ireland Assembly website at http://www.niassembly.gov.uk) or contact an MLA .

question_en: Most citizens of the UK, the Irish republic or the Commonwealth aged 18 or over can stand for public office, except for (choose THREE options):

options.en: People found guilty of certain criminal offences

explanation_en: Members of the Commonwealth aged 18 or over can stand for public office. There are some exceptions, including: members of the armed forces, civil servants and people found guilty of certain criminal offences .

question_en: On which of the following issues the  Northern Ireland Assembly CANNOT make decisions?
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly can make decisions on issues such as education, agriculture, the environment, health and social services. It cannot make decisions on planning issues .

question_en: In which city is the Scottish Parliament building, also known as Holyrood, located?
explanation_en: In Scotland the elected members, called MSPs, meet in the Scottish Parliament building at Holyrood in Edinburgh .

question_en: What name is given to the elected members of the Scottish Parliament?
explanation_en: Elected members of the Scottish Parliament are known as Members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs) .

question_en: The public can listen to debates in the Palace of Westminster from public galleries located in:

options.en: Both, the House of Commons and House of Lords
All of the above are false

explanation_en: The public can listen to debates in the Palace of Westminster from public galleries in both the House of Commons and the House of Lords .

question_en: Which court deals with the most serious cases of children aged 10 to 17 in England, Wales and Northern Ireland?
explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, if an accused person is aged 10 to 17, the case is normally heard in a Youth Court in front of up to three specially trained magistrates or a District Judge. The most serious cases will go the Crown Court .

question_en: When was the National Trust founded?
explanation_en: The National Trust was founded in 1895 by three volunteers.

question_en: Who is married to the King of the United Kingdom?
explanation_en: The King is married to Camilla, the Queen Consort .

question_en: What is the name of the building where the Senedd members meet?
explanation_en: In Wales the elected members, known as SMs, meet in the Senedd building in Cardiff Bay .

question_en: When did the ‘Habeas Corpus Act’ become law?
explanation_en: The Habeas Corpus Act became law in 1679 .

question_en: When was the Treaty of Rome signed?
explanation_en: The European Union (EU), originally called the European Economic Community (EEC), was set up by six western European countries (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands) who signed the Treaty of Rome on the 25th March 1957 .

question_en: The King is the head of state in the UK:
explanation_en: This statement is true .

question_en: How often are the general elections held in the UK?
explanation_en: MPs are elected at a General Election, which is held at least every five years .

question_en: What is the name of the building where the Northern Ireland Assembly members meet?
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Assembly building is known as Stormont .

question_en: Which of the following is NOT a function of the House of Lords?

options.en: Check laws that have been passed by the House of Commons

explanation_en: The House of Lords can suggest amendments or propose new laws, which are then discussed by MPs. The House of Lords checks laws that have been passed by the House of Commons to ensure they are fit for purpose. It also holds the government to account to make sure that it is working in the best interests of the people.

question_en: How is the Speaker elected?

options.en: By other MPs in a secret ballot
By the members of the House of Lords by proportional representation
By the members of the House of Commons in a public ballot

explanation_en: The Speaker is chosen by other MPs in a secret ballot .

question_en: When was the Northern Ireland Parliament established for the first time?
explanation_en: The Northern Ireland Parliament was first established in 1922 .

question_en: What name is given to the system used to deal with children and young people who have committed an offence in Scotland?

options.en: The Sheriff Court for Young Children

explanation_en: In Scotland a system called the Children’s Hearings System is used to deal with children and young people who have committed an offence.

question_en: What is the function of the UN Security Council?

options.en: Recommend action when there are international crises and threats to peace
Recommend humanitarian action when there are natural disasters

explanation_en: The UN Security Council recommends action when there are international crises and threats to peace .

question_en: Which of the following charities works for the prevention of cruelty to children?
explanation_en: The NSPCC is the National Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children.

question_en: What does the term ‘Hansard’ relate to?

options.en: Official reports were proceedings in Parliament are published
A festival which celebrates the founding of the Sikh community

explanation_en: Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on television and published in official reports called Hansard.

question_en: In Wales, the jury has to listen to the evidence presented at the trial and then decide a verdict of ‘guilty’, ‘not guilty’ or ‘not proven’ based of what they have heard:
explanation_en: False . The jury has to listen to the evidence presented at the trial and then decide a verdict of ‘guilty’ or ‘not guilty’ based of what they have heard. In Scotland (not Wales), a third verdict of ‘not proven’ is also possible.

question_en: A National Insurance number does on its own prove to an employer that you have the right to work in the UK:
explanation_en: False . A National Insurance number does not on its own prove to an employer that you have the right to work in the UK.

question_en: When driving in the UK, where do you need to display the tax disc which shows that the annual road tax has been paid?
explanation_en: When driving in the UK, you must pay an annual road tax and display the tax disc, which shows that the tax has been paid, on the windowscreen .

question_en: Which of the following statements is TRUE?

options.en: A jury is made up of members of the public who have applied to be part of it
A jury is made up of members of the public chosen at random from the local electoral register

explanation_en: A jury is made up of members of the public chosen at random from the local electoral register.

question_en: It is a civil offence to cause harassment, alarm or distress to someone because of their religion or ethnic origin:
explanation_en: False . It is a criminal offence to cause harassment, alarm or distress to someone because of their religion or ethnic origin.

question_en: Where can you get contact details for all your representatives and their parties (choose TWO options)?
explanation_en: You can get contact details for all your representatives and their parties from your local library and from http://www.parliament.uk .

question_en: What is the Old Bailey?

options.en: The name of a traditional Welsh cake

explanation_en: The Old Bailey is probably the most famous criminal court in the world .

question_en: What is the relationship between the monarch and the government?

options.en: Approve the government’s decisions on government policies
Advise, warn and encourage the government’s decisions
Advise, encourage and approve the government’s decisions
Advise and approve the government’s decisions

explanation_en: The monarch has regular meetings with the Prime Minister and can advise, warn and encourage, but the decisions on government policies are made by the Prime Minister and cabinet.

question_en: The UK is a ‘constitutional monarchy’:
explanation_en: This statement is true .

question_en: Where can people facing domestic violence get help from (choose TWO options)?
explanation_en: A solicitor or the Citizens Advice Bureau can explain the available options to anyone facing domestic violence.

question_en: People might be taken to court if they owe money to someone:
explanation_en: This statement is true .

question_en: How many members does a jury have in England, Wales and Northern Ireland?
explanation_en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland a jury has 12 members , and in Scotland a jury has 15 members.

question_en: What TWO tests do you need to pass to get a driving licence in the UK?
explanation_en: To get a UK driving licence you must pass a driving test, which tests both your knowledge and your practical skills.

question_en: What is the role of the Speaker?

options.en: To keep order during political debates to make sure the rules are followed
To point out what they see as the government’s failures and weaknesses
To challenge the government and put forward alternative policies

explanation_en: The Speaker keeps order during political debates to make sure the rules are followed .

question_en: Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding Youth Courts?

options.en: The parents or carers of the young person being judged are expected to attend the hearing
Members of the public are not allowed in them
They are used for trials of young people aged 10 to 18
The name or photographs of the accused young person cannot be published in newspapers or used by the media

explanation_en: They are used for trials of young people aged 10 to 17 .

question_en: Who is responsible for the creation of the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms?
explanation_en: The Council of Europe has no power to make laws but draws up conventions and charters, the most well-known of which is the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms.

question_en: Members of the House of Lords may stand for election to the House of Commons and are eligible for all public offices:
explanation_en: False . Members of the House of Lords may not stand for election to the House of Commons but are eligible for all other public offices.

question_en: What does the following term ‘PDSA’ stand for?
explanation_en: PDSA’ stands for People’s Dispensary for Sick Animals .

question_en: The Commonwealth is based on the core values of democracy, good government and the rule of law:
explanation_en: This statement is true .

question_en: How long does it take to donate blood?
explanation_en: Giving blood only takes about an hour to do.

question_en: Where do local authorities get funding from (choose TWO answers)?
explanation_en: Local authorities are funded by money from the central government and by local taxes .

question_en: What does the term MEPs stand for?
explanation_en: MEPs stands for Members of the European Parliament .

question_en: In which of the following matters can the Scottish Parliament legislate (choose FIVE options)?
explanation_en: The Scottish Parliament can legislate on: civil and criminal law, health, education, planning and additional tax-raising powers .

question_en: Drinking in public can be a criminal offence and you can be fined or arrested for it:
explanation_en: This stamens is true .

question_en: Whose details are NOT listed in the Phone Book?
explanation_en: MPs, SMs and MSPs are listed in The Phone Book. The King is not .
